Name: Oritone Delta
Creator: Oritone contributors
Description: Symmetric blue-to-warm diverging map with a high-lightness neutral center. Designed for signed anomalies, residuals, and centered scalar fields.
Reference: Oritone color direction references the Okabe & Ito color-blind-friendly palette.

Name: Oritone Balance
Creator: Oritone contributors
Description: Symmetric teal-to-rose diverging map with equalized endpoint lightness and a warm neutral center. Useful when blue/orange semantics would conflict with the data story.
Reference: Oritone color direction references the Okabe & Ito color-blind-friendly palette.

Name: Oritone Ember
Creator: Oritone contributors
Description: Asymmetric warm sequential map using one dominant amber/ember hue family with monotonic lightness. High contrast without rainbow-like hue jumps.
Reference: Oritone color direction references the Okabe & Ito color-blind-friendly palette.

Name: Oritone Marine
Creator: Oritone contributors
Description: Asymmetric blue-cyan sequential map with monotonic lightness and strong low-to-high contrast. Tuned for continuous fields where a cool scalar scale reads naturally.
Reference: Oritone color direction references the Okabe & Ito color-blind-friendly palette.

Name: Oritone Canopy
Creator: Oritone contributors
Description: Asymmetric blue-green-to-amber sequential map with three dominant color regions and monotonic lightness. Emphasizes mid-to-high scalar structure while preserving order.
Reference: Oritone color direction references the Okabe & Ito color-blind-friendly palette.

Name: Oritone Fusion
Creator: Oritone contributors
Description: Asymmetric multi-hue sequential map moving from muted purple through blue and green into amber. Built for broad dynamic range while keeping a monotonic lightness ramp.
Reference: Oritone color direction references the Okabe & Ito color-blind-friendly palette.
